content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Both amendments identify real identity/reuse defects in `materialize_beads`. One can bind a short explicit `bead_id` to the wrong bead because explicit lookup trusts the first successful `br show`; the other can reuse the same unlabeled existing bead twice in one export because explicit-id hits are not removed from the title-match cache.

## Votes

- `fr-8-355e24d2`: Accept
  `find_explicit_materialized_bead()` iterates `explicit_bead_lookup_candidates()` in raw-id order, so a short explicit id is probed before the milestone-qualified form. On the first successful `br show`, it returns that bead immediately and does not verify that the resolved bead belongs to the current milestone namespace or corresponds to the canonicalized reference. Because plan ids are canonicalized elsewhere and `BeadProposal.bead_id` is documented as a stable id or suffix, this can attach the proposal's dependency/comment updates to the wrong existing bead when the short id resolves elsewhere.
- `fr-8-9993cde1`: Accept
  When explicit-id lookup succeeds, `materialize_bead_entry()` returns before touching `existing_by_title`, so the matched bead remains available for later same-title reuse. That is unsafe because `identity_label_matches()` explicitly treats candidates with no `proposal:*` or `workstream:*` identity label as matching any requested identity label. As a result, a legacy/manual bead with only milestone-scoped labels can satisfy the explicit proposal first and then be selected again for a second same-title proposal, collapsing `planned_bead_id_map` and dependency wiring onto one actual bead.
